summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the CanInstall middleware AJAX bypass allows an unauthenticated attacker to overwrite the superadmin account on a fully installed Bagisto instance by sending a POST request with the X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest header.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the attacker-controlled POST request to bypass the CanInstall middleware and overwrite the superadmin account (id=1) in the admins table, changing the email from admin@example.com to evil@attacker.com.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh builds the Docker image, installs Bagisto, marks it as installed, then sends a POST request to /install/api/admin-config-setup with the X-Requested-With header. The admin account email in the database changes from admin@example.com to evil@attacker.com, confirming the middleware bypass.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks the admins table in MySQL before and after the exploit. It confirms the admin email changed to the attacker-controlled value evil@attacker.com, proving the CanInstall middleware bypass and admin account takeover.
evidence_produced_by_run: The original admin email (admin@example.com) was recorded before the exploit.
The POST /install/api/admin-config-setup request with X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest returned HTTP 200.
After the exploit, the admins table shows email=evil@attacker.com for id=1, confirming the superadmin account was overwritten.
The application was marked as fully installed (storage/installed file exists) before the exploit was executed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
